threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You keep asking for the 'nearest tube' at every bus stop, like there’s supposed to be one around the corner.
Caught a night bus from Brixton to Clapham and a guy was playing air guitar to the driver’s tunes. Best part? Everyone joined in by the last stop.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I heard a group of kids arguing passionately about the best kebab spot on Hermannstraße—now that's local culture.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a kebab shop that serves its döner with a side of funky techno beats. Late-night bites never felt so alive.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local if they don't flinch at the BART delays but still complain about the taco truck line at 10 PM on a Friday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni delayed at Civic Center yesterday—an entire opera troupe got off, all in costume, singing their way down the street. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the fountains in Plaça de la Vila are just for decoration, but locals fill their water bottles daily. It's our secret hydration spot.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll catch them staring at the tanned locals playing petanca at Parc de l'Estrella, trying to figure out if it's a sport or just another casual hangout.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of just 'the train'—everyone else knows it’s not special enough for that.
Last night on the 196, saw a guy break into a full Shakespeare monologue. No one batted an eye, just carried on scrolling on their phones.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw someone feeding a stray cat while arguing with a friend about the best Kebab in town—it felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a club that doubles as a swimming pool. Dance all night, then take a dip at 4 AM. It's wild!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they grimace at a BART delay, yet still confidently navigate the bus detours like a GPS whisperer.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy doing yoga in the middle of the Muni aisle while we were stuck in traffic. Only in SF do you see that kind of calm amid chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think they can grab a quick bite at a bocadillo bar, but locals know the best sandwiches are from the little bakery near Plaça de la Vila.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they insist on ordering 'pan con tomate' at every meal, like it’s the holy grail of tapas.
